description: DELETE /v1/devices/:id — role: viewer/owner. Revokes a device AND force-closes every live WS for that device in the same operation (v1-architecture.md §10.2, owner ruling B4). Matches openapi.yaml #/paths/~1v1~1devices~1{id}/delete.

side_effect_force_close.description: In the same operation, for every socket in ctx.getWebSockets('dev_9c4e2b7a1f'), the server sends this error envelope and then closes the connection (WS close code 1008). This is what the revoked device sees on its still-open socket — it does NOT get to keep streaming until its next HTTP request.

notes: unauthenticated is the code proto/realtime/SPEC.md §13 reserves for exactly 'the connection's credential became invalid mid-connection (e.g. revoked)': retryable=false, fatal=true (closes the connection).
The device's subsequent HTTP requests also 401 (its token hash no longer resolves to a live devices row) — revocation cuts it off on both transports at once.
Caller here is the owner device from create-space.json revoking the viewer device from join.json.
